title: Cathryn Clüver Ashbrook on Office Hours
summary: Cathryn Clüver Ashbrook (@Cluverc), Executive Director of the Belfer Center’s Future of Diplomacy Project and the newly created Project on Europe and the Transatlantic Relationship, speaks with Aroop Mukharji (@aroopmukharji) about the future of the U.S.-Europe relationship, how language can influence policy, and the benefits of dual citizenship.
authors: Eric Rosenbach, Carlo Giannone, Slavina Ancheva, Luc Hillion, Ethan Lee
body: Gallery 1 of 4 Cathryn Clüver Ashbrook: Future of the European Union 2 of 4 Cathryn Clüver Ashbrook: How Language Can Influence Thinking 3 of 4 Cathryn Clüver Ashbrook: Transatlanticism’s Shaky Foundation 4 of 4 Cathryn Clüver Ashbrook: The Benefits of Dual Citizenship Cathryn Clüver Ashbrook (@Cluverc), Executive Director of the Belfer Center’s Future of Diplomacy Project and the newly created Project on Europe and the Transatlantic Relationship, speaks with Aroop Mukharji (@aroopmukharji) about the future of the U.S.-Europe relationship, how language can influence policy, and the benefits of dual citizenship. _list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: In this interview, Cathryn Clüver Ashbrook, Executive Director of the Belfer Center's Future of Diplomacy Project and Project on Europe and the Transatlantic Relationship, discusses key issues in transatlantic affairs. The conversation covers the future of the U.S.-Europe relationship, exploring the shaky foundations of transatlanticism in the contemporary geopolitical context. Ashbrook also delves into how language and framing can influence policy thinking and decision-making processes. Additionally, she examines the future trajectory of the European Union and highlights the personal and political benefits of dual citizenship in an increasingly interconnected world. The discussion provides insights from a diplomatic and strategic studies perspective on navigating complex international partnerships.
result.main_content_zh: 在此次访谈中，贝尔弗中心未来外交项目及欧洲与跨大西洋关系项目执行主任卡特琳·克利弗·阿什布鲁克探讨了跨大西洋事务中的关键议题。对话涉及美欧关系的未来，审视了当代地缘政治背景下跨大西洋主义的脆弱基础。阿什布鲁克还深入分析了语言和叙事框架如何影响政策思维和决策过程。此外，她探讨了欧盟的未来发展轨迹，并强调了在日益互联的世界中双重国籍带来的个人与政治益处。本次讨论从外交和战略研究的角度，为应对复杂的国际伙伴关系提供了见解。
